channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How AI is helping improve heart health in rural Australia
item.description: A doctor is sitting across a desk from a patient. The doctor is holding a tablet and a pen. Medical charts renderings are in the background.

item.title: Your File Upload Endpoint Is Part of Your Attack Surface
item.description: Your File Upload Endpoint Is Part of Your Attack Surface Most file upload flows look safe at first glance. You check the extension. You validate the Content-Type header. You set a file size limit....

item.title: Why Your Code is Slow: A Practical Guide to Algorithms and Big O Notation
item.description: If you’re a Software Development student and they’ve given you the Algorithms unit to work on, then you’re probably having a problem by now. It’s not enough to simply get your code working to pass...

item.title: Register a User and Test API using Postman in Next.js with MongoDB
item.description: When building a full stack application, one common feature is user registration. This means allowing users to create an account using email and password. In this blog we will understand how to: ✔...

item.title: Event Sourcing: The Record Statement You Never Throw Away
item.description: Your bank doesn't show you just your current balance. It shows you every deposit, every withdrawal, every transfer — in order, forever. Not because storage is cheap, but because the history IS the...

item.title: Emerging Hardware & The Spatial Web: A 2026 Guide to Networking the Metaverse
item.description: IT InstaTunnel Team Published by our engineering team Emerging Hardware & The Spatial Web: A 2026 Guide to Networking the Metaverse Emerging Hardware & The Spatial Web: A 2026 Guide to Networking...

item.title: Upgrade Umbraco 13 to 17: Property Editors + Property Value Converters
item.description: This is part five in a series about common tasks you'll encounter when upgrading Umbraco 13 to 17. In this part, we’ll look at updating Property Editors and Property Value Converters. When...

item.title: When Spreadsheets Stop Working: The Awkward Middle Between Sheets and ERP
item.description: Most small manufacturing shops start on spreadsheets because that is the obvious move. They are fast, flexible, familiar, and free. If you are building a small operation and just trying to keep...

item.title: The Day Coding Stopped Being the Hard Part
item.description: Not long ago, writing code was the hardest part of building software. You had to search documentation, piece together examples, debug strange errors, and slowly move from idea to implementation....

item.title: Vitalik Sanctuary Tech Vision, EOA Path for AA, Programmable Smart Accounts, ERC-8004 Agent Stack
item.description: We are welcoming you to our weekly digest! Here, we discuss the latest trends and advancements in account abstraction, chain abstraction and everything related, as well as bring some insights from...

item.title: The Smart Contract Fuzzer Showdown: Foundry vs Echidna vs Medusa vs Trident (2026 Benchmark)
item.description: The Smart Contract Fuzzer Showdown: Foundry vs Echidna vs Medusa vs Trident (2026 Benchmark) Which fuzzer actually catches the bugs that matter? I tested all four on real DeFi vulnerability...

item.title: The Download: Early adopters cash in on China’s OpenClaw craze, and US batteries slump
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze In...

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot Health can connect to your medical records and wearables
item.description: Microsoft announced on Thursday that it's launching Copilot Health, a "separate, secure space" in Copilot for asking questions about lab results and medical records, searching for providers,...

item.title: Google is using old news reports and AI to predict flash floods
item.description: A new way to solve data scarcity: Turning qualitative reports into quantitative data with an LLM.

item.title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
item.description: The impact of artificial intelligence extends far beyond the digital world and into our everyday lives, across the cars we drive, the appliances in our homes, and medical devices that keep people...

item.title: Google Maps is getting an AI ‘Ask Maps’ feature and upgraded ‘immersive’ navigation
item.description: The tech giant says the "Immersive Navigation" launch is the biggest update to Maps in over a decade.

item.title: Google Maps Gets Chatty With a New Gemini-Powered Interface
item.description: “Ask Maps,” rolling out today to Google Maps on mobile, lets you ask Gemini questions about locations and even to plan trips on your behalf.

item.title: You can now ask Google Maps ‘complex, real-world questions’ — and Gemini will answer
item.description: Google is continuing to weave Gemini into the firmament of its most-used products. Today, it announced that Google Maps was getting a new AI-powered "Ask Maps" feature that allows for "complex,...

item.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer turns your spare Mac into an AI agent
item.description: Perplexity wants to be more than just an answer engine. On Wednesday, it launched Personal Computer, a new AI agent tool that can turn a spare Mac into a locally run AI system, pitching it as "a...

item.title: JBL’s new party speaker comes with a karaoke mic that helps you hit those high notes
item.description: Karaoke is more fun for everyone when the person holding the mic sounds as good as a song's original performer. So the latest addition to JBL's PartyBox Bluetooth speaker line, the On-the-Go 2...

item.title: Attackers Don't Just Send Phishing Emails. They Weaponize Your SOC's Workload
item.description: The most dangerous phishing campaigns aren’t just designed to fool employees. Many are designed to exhaust the analysts investigating them. When a phishing investigation takes 12 hours instead of...

item.title: Iran-linked cyber attack targets US medtech giant Stryker
item.description: US medical equipment provider Stryker said its global networks were disrupted by a cyberattack on Wednesday, allegedly carried out by a hacking group linked to Iran. The attack impacted Stryker's...

item.title: Brutal times for the US battery industry
item.description: Just a few years ago, the battery industry was hot, hot, hot. There was a seemingly infinite number of companies popping up, with shiny new chemistries and massive fundraising rounds. My biggest...

item.title: Apple Issues Security Updates for Older iOS Devices Targeted by Coruna WebKit Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday backported fixes for a security flaw in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS Sonoma to older versions after it was found to be used as part of the Coruna exploit kit. The vulnerability,...

item.title: Six Android Malware Families Target Pix Payments, Banking Apps, and Crypto Wallets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered half-a-dozen new Android malware families that come with capabilities to steal data from compromised devices and conduct financial fraud. The Android...

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited n8n RCE Bug as 24,700 Instances Remain Exposed
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical security flaw impacting n8n to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence...

item.title: How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II
AI ‘actor’ Tilly Norwood put out the worst song I’ve ever heard
item.description: This song is an AI actor's rallying cry to other AI actors, urging them to keep going despite the naysayers who doubt their humanity. Literally no one can relate to this.

item.title: One of Grammarly’s ‘experts’ is suing the company over its identity-stealing AI feature
item.description: For months, Grammarly has been using the identities of real people (including us) for its "Expert Review" AI suggestions without getting their permission, and now it's facing a lawsuit from one of...

item.title: Ford’s new AI assistant will help fleet owners know if seatbelts are being used
item.description: Ford Pro AI debuted at Work Truck Week in Indianapolis and is now available to all of its U.S.-based Pro telematics subscribers.
